  1. A heterogeneous solution is one that has a uniform composition throughout the body of the solution.
  2. A homogeneous solution is a mixture in which solute and solvent are present in two or more phases.
  3. In a solution, a component present in a smaller proportion is called a solvent.
  4. In the aqueous solution of salt, salt is the solute and water is the solvent.
